verdicts.note: The record claims exists='yes' with url='https://mcp.facebook.com/ads', but that URL is NOT found in any evidence. Evidence shows only open-source third-party MCP servers (pipeboard-co/meta-ads-mcp on GitHub). There is no official Meta-hosted MCP server documented. This should be exists='no'.

The URL https://mcp.facebook.com/ads does not appear anywhere in the evidence. The only MCP server documented is https://github.com/pipeboard-co/meta-ads-mcp, which is a third-party open-source implementation, not an official Meta MCP server.
Flag 'official_meta_mcp_exists' is false—no evidence shows Meta offers an official MCP server. Only third-party open-source implementations exist (pipeboard-co/meta-ads-mcp).
Evidence confirms OAuth2 with access tokens (user, system user, or page tokens) passed as query param or Bearer header, requiring ads_management/ads_read scopes.
Evidence confirms Development Access is self-serve but limited; Standard/Advanced Access requires Meta App Review, business verification, and use-case approval—matching 'admin-approval'.
Evidence confirms REST API (Graph API-based Marketing API) with broad coverage (campaigns, ad sets, ads, creatives, insights, audiences) and official PHP SDK.
Evidence confirms Development Access is self-serve with heavy rate limits (60 points/300s), and production requires App Review. 'buildable-with-caveats' is accurate.
Evidence confirms App Review and business verification required for production Standard/Advanced Access; dev tier is limited to 60 points/300s and test accounts only.

revised.name: Meta Ads
revised.one_liner: Meta Ads (Marketing API) lets developers programmatically manage Facebook/Instagram advertising campaigns via a broad REST API secured by OAuth2, free to use but gated by tiered rate limits requiring Meta App Review for production-scale access.

auth_methods.notes: User, System User, or Page access tokens with ads_management/ads_read scopes; passed as access_token query param or Authorization: Bearer header.

revised.self_serve_notes: Development Access tier is self-serve with a Facebook developer app (limited quota, 60 points/300s), but production-grade Standard/Advanced Access requires Meta App Review, business verification, and use-case approval.

api_surface.notes: Graph API-based Marketing API covering campaigns, ad sets, ads, creatives, insights, audiences, etc. Also has an official PHP Business SDK and other language SDKs.

revised.main_blocker: Full production access requires Meta App Review and business verification; default dev access is heavily rate-limited (60 points/300s) and only usable for testing on owned accounts.

evidence.supports: OAuth2 auth details, scopes, rate limits, REST API surface
Official SDK usage with app ID/secret/access token
App Review process required for Advanced Access
Tiered access model: Development, Standard, Advanced
Existence of open-source Meta Ads MCP servers
